general.title: General Settings
general.zoomDescription: Adjusts the zoom level to make content easier to read.
general.autoUpdateDescription: This will automatically update the app when new versions are available.
general.releaseChannelDescription: Controls which update channel the app uses.
general.runtimeModeDescription: Select the runtime to use for running the app.
general.runtimeModeSwitchDescription: Switching runtime mode will stop your currently running app.
general.cloudSandboxDescription: Cloud Sandbox runs previews remotely and gives you a shareable preview link. Note: running in cloud mode consumes Pro credits.
general.restartRequired: You will need to restart Dyad for your settings to take effect.
general.stableChannelDescription: You'll stay on your current version until a newer stable release is available, or you can manually downgrade now.
general.cloudSandboxProDescription: Cloud sandboxes are a Dyad Pro feature.
general.nodeConfigured: Node.js is properly configured and ready to use.
general.nodeSelectFolder: Select the folder where Node.js is installed if it's not in your system PATH.
general.systemNodeTooOldDescription: Dyad requires system Node.js v20 or newer, or a managed runtime.
general.nodePathNotFound: Could not find Node.js at the path "{{path}}"
general.runtimePreferenceUpdateFailed: Failed to update Node.js runtime preference: {{message}}

workflow.title: Workflow Settings
workflow.defaultChatModeDescription: Controls the default chat mode when you open a new chat.
workflow.autoApproveDescription: This will automatically approve code changes and run them.
workflow.autoFixProblemsDescription: This will automatically fix TypeScript errors in Build mode. In Agent mode, the agent runs its own type checks instead.
workflow.appBlueprintDescription: When creating a new app, generate a lightweight app blueprint (name, design, color, template) before building.
workflow.autoExpandPreviewDescription: Automatically expand the preview panel when code changes are made.
workflow.keepPreviewsRunningDescription: Note: this may take more memory but allows faster preview loads when switching apps.
workflow.chatEventNotificationDescription: Show native notifications when a chat response completes or a questionnaire needs your input while the app is not focused.
workflow.autoApproveSafeMcpTools: Skip consent for safe MCP tools
workflow.autoApproveSafeMcpToolsPro: Skip consent for safe MCP tools (Pro)
workflow.autoApproveSafeMcpToolsDescription: In Agent mode, use a fast model to judge each MCP tool call and skip the consent prompt for ones that look safe. Risky actions (deleting data, sending messages, changing access) still ask.
workflow.autoApproveNonSchemaSqlDescription: In Agent mode, skip the consent prompt when running SQL that does not change the database schema. Schema changes still require approval.
workflow.autoApproveDisabledHint: You can disable auto-approve in the Settings.
workflow.autoFixProblemsDisabledHint: You can disable Auto-fix problems in the Settings page.
workflow.blockUnsafeNpmPackagesDescription: Uses socket.dev to detect unsafe packages and blocks them
workflow.cloudSandboxDescription: Run your app on the Cloud (more secure and uses less local system resources. Note: using Cloud resources consumes Pro credits)
workflow.cloudSandboxStillActive: Cloud Sandbox is still active for the current app. Switch the runtime mode back to Local to fully turn it off.

ai.title: AI Settings
ai.thinkingBudgetDescription: Controls how long the AI model can think. Higher budgets mean more thorough reasoning.
ai.maxChatTurnsDescription: Limits how many recent chat turns are included in context.
ai.maxToolCallStepsDescription: Limits how many tool calls the agent can make before pausing.
ai.connectCustomEndpoint: Connect to a custom LLM API endpoint
ai.deleteProviderConfirmation: This will permanently delete this custom provider and all its associated models. This action cannot be undone.
ai.freeTierDescription: Free tiers typically allow the provider to use your data to train AI models.
ai.apiKeyVerificationMessage: Dyad could not verify this {{name}} API key.
ai.apiKeyCannotTest: {{name}} API keys cannot be tested yet.
ai.readyDescription: You can now start building with Dyad.
ai.providerNotFoundDescription: The provider with ID "{{provider}}" could not be found.
ai.contextCompactionDescription: Automatically compact long conversations to stay within context limits. Original messages are preserved in the app data directory.
ai.chatTurnEconomyDescription: Minimal context to reduce token usage and improve response times.
ai.chatTurnDefaultDescription: Balanced context size for most conversations.
ai.chatTurnPlusDescription: Slightly higher context size for detailed conversations.
ai.chatTurnHighDescription: Extended context for complex conversations requiring more history.
ai.chatTurnMaxDescription: Maximum context (not recommended due to cost and speed).
ai.toolStepsLowDescription: Limits tool calls to 25. Good for simple tasks that don't need many steps.
ai.toolStepsHighDescription: Extended limit for complex multi-step tasks (may increase cost and time).
ai.thinkingEffortTooltip: Effort: how hard the model thinks before responding. Applies to reasoning-capable models.
ai.zoomOptionDescription90: Slightly zoomed out to fit more content on screen.
ai.zoomOptionDescription110: Zoom in a little for easier reading.
ai.requiresProOrApiKey: {{model}} — requires Dyad Pro or an API key from {{provider}}
ai.dataSharingDescription: Data may be shared with the AI provider and used for training models.
ai.trialUpgradeDescription: Upgrade from Dyad Pro trial to unlock more models.
ai.ensureModelsLoaded: Ensure {{provider}} is running and models are loaded.
ai.unlockAllModels: Unlock all models with Dyad Pro
ai.useModelWithOwnKey: Use {{model}} with your own {{provider}} API key
ai.freeModelOwnAccount: Free models run through your own {{provider}} account. Add an API key in provider settings to use this model.
ai.proModelAccessDescription: Dyad Pro gives you {{model}} and every other leading AI model with one subscription — no API keys needed.
ai.useOwnProviderApiKey: Or use your own {{provider}} API key

telemetry.title: Telemetry
telemetry.description: This records anonymous usage data to improve the product.
telemetry.privacyNotice: We use anonymous telemetry data to improve the product. No personal data is collected.
telemetry.helpImprove: Help improve Dyad with anonymous usage data.
telemetry.noCodeOrMessages: Note: this does not log your code or messages.

agentPermissions.title: Agent Permissions (Pro)
agentPermissions.description: Configure permissions for Agent built-in tools.

toolsMcp.title: Tools (MCP)
toolsMcp.name: Name
toolsMcp.keyValueRequired: Both key and value are required
toolsMcp.duplicateKey: Environment variable with this key already exists

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.resetDescription: This will delete all your apps, chats, and settings. This action cannot be undone.
dangerZone.resetConfirmation: Are you sure you want to reset everything? This will delete all your apps, chats, and settings. This action cannot be undone.
dangerZone.resetSuccess: Successfully reset everything. Restart the application.

apiKey.overrideEnvVar: Setting a key here will override the environment variable (if set).
apiKey.envVarActive: This key is currently active (no settings key set).
apiKey.envVarOverridden: This key is currently being overridden by the key set in Settings.
apiKey.envVarNotSetDescription: The {{name}} environment variable is not set.
apiKey.envVarHelpText: This key is set outside the application. If present, it will be used only if no key is configured in the Settings section above. Requires app restart to detect changes.
apiKey.pasteHint: Copied your API key? Click to paste & save it

azure.configuredDescription: Dyad will use the credentials saved in Settings for Azure OpenAI models.
azure.usingEnvVarsDescription: AZURE_API_KEY and AZURE_RESOURCE_NAME are set. Values saved below will override them.
azure.configRequiredDescription: Provide your Azure resource name and API key below, or configure the AZURE_API_KEY and AZURE_RESOURCE_NAME environment variables.
azure.apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter your Azure OpenAI API key
azure.configNeededDescription: Azure OpenAI requests require both a resource name and API key. Enter them above or supply the environment variables instead.
azure.envVarsHelpText: You can continue to configure Azure via environment variables. If both variables are present and no settings are saved, Dyad will use them automatically.
azure.envVarsPrecedence: Values saved in Settings take precedence over environment variables. Restart Dyad after changing environment variables.

vertex.locationHelp: If you see a "model not found" error, try a different region. Some partner models (MaaS) are only available in specific locations (e.g., us-central1, us-west2).
vertex.serviceAccountKeyPlaceholder: Paste the full JSON contents of your service account key here
vertex.configRequiredDescription: Provide Project, Location, and a service account JSON key with Vertex AI access.

models.title: Models
models.description: Manage specific models available through this provider.
models.noCustomModels: No custom models have been added for this provider yet.
models.deleteConfirmTitle: Are you sure you want to delete this model?
models.deleteConfirmDescription: This action cannot be undone. This will permanently delete the custom model "{{name}}" (API Name: {{apiName}}).
models.invalidMaxOutput: Max Output Tokens must be a valid number
models.invalidContextWindow: Context Window must be a valid number
models.addModelDescription: Configure a new language model for the selected provider.
models.editModelDescription: Modify the configuration of the selected language model.
models.modelIdHelp: This must match the model expected by the API

customProvider.addDescription: Connect to a custom language model provider API.
customProvider.editDescription: Update your custom language model provider configuration.
customProvider.providerIdHelp: A unique identifier for this provider (no spaces).
customProvider.displayNameHelp: The name that will be displayed in the UI.
customProvider.apiBaseUrlHelp: The base URL for the API endpoint.
customProvider.envVarHelp: Environment variable name for the API key.

page.advancedDescription: We recommend keeping the default settings unless something is not working
page.sandboxScriptDescription: Allow local-agent attachment scripts to inspect files with execute_sandbox_script.
page.experimentsDescription: We do not recommend enabling experiments as these features may not be stable
page.mcpToolSearchDescription: When many MCP tools are enabled, let the agent search for the tools on demand instead of listing every tool in its context. Requires sandbox script execution.
page.mcpToolSearchDisabled: Cannot be enabled unless sandbox script execution is on.
page.pnpmWarningDescription: Show the pnpm release-age warning toast and one-click pnpm upgrade action.
page.enableSelectApp: Enable Select App from Home Chat Input
page.selectAppDescription: Show an app selector in the home chat input to start a chat referencing an existing app.
page.codeExplorerDescription: Let the local agent explore configured TypeScript projects with a compiler-backed code graph.
